description:
You are acting as a junior storage-administrator who has just been asked to test a tiny linear-programming model that management will eventually integrate into an automated “what-to-archive” tool.  
A ready-made LP file is already present on the system at  

    /home/user/storage_problem.lp  

Your assignment:

1. Solve that model with the command-line LP solver that is available in the container.  
2. Create a plain-text log called  

    /home/user/storage_solution.log  

3. The log **must contain exactly four lines, in this precise order and format (no extra spaces, tabs, or blank lines):**  
   objective=<numeric_value>  
   x1=<numeric_value>  
   x2=<numeric_value>  
   x3=<numeric_value>  

   • Use only digits for the values (they are all integers in this model).  
   • The key words (objective, x1, x2, x3) are all lowercase.  
   • The equal sign “=” must be used with no spaces on either side.  
   • Do **not** include anything else in the file—no headings, no explanatory text, no trailing blank line.

4. Place the file exactly at the path listed above.  
5. When the file is finished, you are done; no additional output is required.

Hints (not mandatory):
• A single terminal command is enough: run the solver, capture its standard output, and post-process it with text-filtering utilities to extract just the optimal objective value and the three variable assignments, redirecting the result into the target log file.  
• Make sure the numeric values you copy into the log correspond to the optimal solution that the solver reports, not to intermediate or non-optimal iterations.

The automated tests will confirm:
• The file /home/user/storage_solution.log exists.  
• It contains exactly four newline-separated lines in the format described above.  
• The numeric values match the optimal solution of the model in /home/user/storage_problem.lp.
truth:
Initial files present before the agent starts:

1. /home/user/storage_problem.lp

Contents of /home/user/storage_problem.lp (UNIX LF endings):

Minimize
 obj: x1 + 2 x2 + 3 x3
Subject To
 c1: x1 + x2 + x3 >= 10
Bounds
 0 <= x1 <= 4
 0 <= x2 <= 5
 0 <= x3 <= 7
Generals
 x1 x2 x3
End

Solver availability: the container has the GLPK command-line solver `glpsol` in its PATH.

Required end-state after the agent completes the task:

1. A file exists at /home/user/storage_solution.log with **exactly** the following four lines (including newline characters “\n” after each line and *no* trailing blank line):

objective=17
x1=4
x2=5
x3=1

2. No other files are created or modified outside /home/user unless they are temporary and automatically cleaned up.

The automated grading script will:
• Check that /home/user/storage_solution.log exists.  
• Verify it contains precisely four lines.  
• Verify the literal text and numbers match the lines above.  
• Reject if there are additional spaces, missing newlines, extra lines, or incorrect numbers.
